title: ginkgo testing: fix podman usernamespace join
body:
When there is a podman pause process running the local podman ginkgo tests will join the usernamespace. This because pkg/rootless will automatically join the ns on startup when possible. To fix this we need to use the remote build tag which disables that behavior.

However since the remote tag is also used in the e2e test itself we would always run remote tests which is wrong, this is fixed by using a new `remote_testing` tag for the test.

see discussion here: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/16309#discussion_r1006166930

So... how did these tests ever work in CI?

> So... how did these tests ever work in CI?

It does not really matter if we are in the user namespace or not, it only matters for calls like getuid() or calls that require extra capabilities.

My question is: how did anything ever work in CI? On my laptop, I invoke ginkgo, and it (mistakenly) thinks that I'm running rootful. But in CI, `skipifrootless` and `skipifremote` work as they should (I've confirmed by downloading all `int` logs from this PR and from non-this-PR, writing a script that parses skips and colorizes (green/red) remote/rootless. I don't see any change between this PR and main. I'm really confused.)

> My question is: how did anything ever work in CI? On my laptop, I invoke ginkgo, and it (mistakenly) thinks that I'm running rootful. But in CI, `skipifrootless` and `skipifremote` work as they should (I've confirmed by downloading all `int` logs from this PR and from non-this-PR, writing a script that parses skips and colorizes (green/red) remote/rootless. I don't see any change between this PR and main. I'm really confused.)

b) on this reply https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/16309#discussion_r1006166930
If there is no podman pause process it cannot join the ns so it will work in CI. If you run a podman command before it will break.

Ahhhh.... thank you, I think I finally got it. The missing piece (for me) was: 
* the podman pause process is now called **catatonit**. `ps auxww|grep pause` or `|grep podman` will not find it.

UPDATE: indeed, killing this process makes ginkgo work (as in, understand that I'm running rootless).

OK, given that understanding, ... I find the `remote` and `remote_testing` tags here confusing, and that's even after spending a lot of time headbanging on them. Is there any way to keep `remote` with its current meaning, and add a new `dontjoinpodmannamespace` tag? Or, even better, tell ginkgo that it should never ever join a podman namespace, that it should clean-start every time? (Sorry, I don't know enough about Go or namespaces to phrase this question correctly)

> OK, given that understanding, ... I find the `remote` and `remote_testing` tags here confusing, and that's even after spending a lot of time headbanging on them. Is there any way to keep `remote` with its current meaning, and add a new `dontjoinpodmannamespace` tag? Or, even better, tell ginkgo that it should never ever join a podman namespace, that it should clean-start every time? (Sorry, I don't know enough about Go or namespaces to phrase this question correctly)

We could add a new tag like that I assume. The main point is that `remote` must stay as it since this is used outside from our control (users of pkg/bindings for example or really anybody who is not using our Makefile).

>The main point is that remote must stay as it [is]

Now I'm confused again. Doesn't this PR change the meaning (and usage) of `remote`?

No I add a new build tag only for testing which makes more sense since we can change those at will without having to worry about breaking any users of the code. ginkgo is now always started with `remote` to prevent the join user namespace problem, since the test where using the same `remote` build tag this caused them to always run in remote mode. The easy fix is to just change this because I am confident that it will not break anything elsewhere.
